content:
{
  "title": "Semantic Contextual Anchoring: Improving LLM Code Generation via Targeted Example Clustering",
  "problem": "LLM code generation can be inconsistent and error-prone, especially when prompt examples are not closely aligned with the target task or lack semantic coherence. Existing prompt selection methods often rely on random or superficial similarity rather than deeper functional relevance.",
  "motivation": "Performance of LLMs on code generation tasks is highly sensitive to prompt construction. With growing model sizes and diverse code domains, systematically improving prompt relevance can yield significant gains. Recent findings reveal that example selection and prompt context quality have outsized effects on output accuracy, making precise prompt engineering more urgent.",
  "method": "We propose Semantic Contextual Anchoring (SCA), a prompting method that clusters candidate prompt examples using a code-focused embedding model (e.g., CodeBERT) to identify clusters of semantically and functionally similar code snippets. For each test case, the prompt dynamically selects in-context examples from the closest cluster, ensuring contextual alignment. The method optionally augments prompts with concise natural-language summaries of example intent, further anchoring the model's attention. This approach aims to maximize semantic coherence between in-context examples and generation targets.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate SCA on standard code generation benchmarks such as HumanEval and MBPP. Baselines will include random example selection, similarity-based retrieval, and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will include pass@k, BLEU, and functional correctness via automated testing. Ablation studies will assess the effect of clustering granularity and summary augmentation."
}
